en.extract:
The plains lark (Corypha kabalii) is a species of lark in the family Alaudidae found in Gabon, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Angola and northwest Zambia. It was formerly treated as a subspecies of the rufous-naped lark.


Taxonomy
The plains lark was formally described in 1943 by the English ornithologist Charles M.N. White based on a specimen collected near Balovale (now the town of Zambezi) in northwest Zambia near the border with Angola. He considered it to be a subspecies of the rufous-naped lark and coined the trinomial name Mirafra africana kabalii. He chose the specific epithet to honour a local collector and hunter Kabali Muzeya. The plains lark is now treated as a separate species and placed in the genus Corypha. This is based on the results of two molecular phylogenetic studies by a team of ornithologists led by Per Alström that were published in 2023 and 2024.
Three subspecies are recognised:

C. k. malbranti (Chapin, 1946) – Gabon to south DR Congo
C. k. kabalii (White, CMN, 1943) – northeast Angola and northwest Zambia
C. k. irwini (da Rosa Pinto, 1968) – southeast Angola (previously synonymized with C. africana pallida)


References

The '''plains lark''' ('''''Corypha kabalii''''') is a species of [[lark]] in the family [[Alaudidae]] found in Gabon, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Angola and northwest Zambia. It was formerly treated as a [[subspecies]] of the [[rufous-naped lark]].

==Taxonomy==
The plains lark was [[species description|formally described]] in 1943 by the English ornithologist Charles M.N. White based on a specimen collected near Balovale (now the town of [[Zambezi, Zambia|Zambezi]]) in northwest Zambia near the border with Angola. He considered it to be a [[subspecies]] of the [[rufous-naped lark]] and coined the [[trinomial name]] ''Mirafra africana kabalii''.<ref>{{ cite journal | last=White | first= | author-link=Hubert Lynes | year=1943 | title=Three new races from northern Rhodesia | journal=Bulletin of the British Ornithologists' Club | volume=64 | page=19-22 [20-21] | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/40587458 }}</ref><ref>{{ cite book | editor1-last=Mayr | editor1-first=Ernst | editor1-link=Ernst Mayr | editor2-last=Greenway | editor2-first=James C. Jr | year=1960 | title=Check-List of Birds of the World | volume=9 | publisher=Museum of Comparative Zoology  | location=Cambridge, Massachusetts | page=11 | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/14480686 }}</ref> He chose the specific epithet to honour a local collector and hunter Kabali Muzeya.<ref>{{ cite web | last=Jobling | first=James A. | title=kabalii | work=The Key to Scientific Names | url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/key-to-scientific-names/search?q=kabalii | publisher=Cornell Lab of Ornithology | access-date=12 September 2024 }}</ref> The plains lark is now treated as a separate species and placed in the genus ''[[Corypha (bird)|Corypha]]''. This is based on the results of two [[molecular phylogenetic]] studies by a team of ornithologists led by [[Per Alström]] that were published in 2023 and 2024.<ref>{{Cite journal | last1=Alström | first1=P. | author1-link=Per Alström | last2=Mohammadi | first2=Z. | last3=Enbody | first3=E.D. | last4=Irestedt | first4=M. | last5=Engelbrecht | first5=D. | last6=Crochet | first6=P.-A. | last7=Guillaumet | first7=A. | last8=Rancilhac | first8=L. | last9=Tieleman | first9=B.I. | last10=Olsson | first10=U. | last11=Donald | first11=P.F. | last12=Stervander | first12=M. | date=2023 | title=Systematics of the avian family Alaudidae using multilocus and genomic data | journal=Avian Research | volume=14 | article-number=100095 | doi=10.1016/j.avrs.2023.100095 | doi-access=free| hdl=11370/bca6ef93-5fe0-40f0-8354-558e15c425fd | hdl-access=free }}</ref><ref name=alstrom2024>{{Cite journal | last1=Alström | first1=P. | last2=Mohammadi | first2=Z. | last3=Donald | first3=P.F. | last4=Nymark | first4=M. | last5=Enbody | first5=E.D. | last6=Irestedt | first6=M. | last7=Elisha | first7=E.B. | last8=Ndithia | first8=H.K. | last9=Tieleman | first9=B.I. | last10=Engelbrecht | first10=D. | last11=Olsson | first11=U. | last12=Rancilhac | first12=L. | last13=Stervander | first13=M. | date=2024 | title=Integrative taxonomy reveals unrecognised species diversity in African ''Corypha'' larks (Aves: Alaudidae) | journal=Zoological Journal of the Linnean Society | volume=200 | issue=4 | pages=1080-1108 | doi=10.1093/zoolinnean/zlad107| hdl=11370/4223d673-4ece-4b55-8be0-2bca4784c33c | hdl-access=free }}</ref><ref name=ioc>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor1-link=Frank Gill (ornithologist) | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | editor3-last=Rasmussen | editor3-first=Pamela | editor3-link=Pamela C. Rasmussen | date=August 2024 | title=Nicators, Bearded Reedling, larks | work=IOC World Bird List Version 14.2 | url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/larks/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union | access-date=12 September 2024 }}</ref>

Three subspecies are recognised:<ref name=ioc/>
* ''C. k. malbranti'' ([[James Chapin|Chapin]], 1946) – Gabon to south DR Congo
* ''C. k. kabalii'' (White, CMN, 1943) – northeast Angola and northwest Zambia
* ''C. k. irwini'' (da Rosa Pinto, 1968) – southeast Angola (previously [[Synonym (taxonomy)|synonymized]] with ''C. africana pallida'')
